speaker: Kaja Kallas
text: The current international order's core failure is the absence of accountability — the rules and UN Charter principles exist and most countries accept them, but there is no functioning mechanism to hold violators, including powerful states, to account.
context: Kallas argues the Security Council is not working as intended and the UN no longer reflects the world as it currently is; she frames reform as developing accountability further, the way the UN improved on the League of Nations.

addressed_in_artifacts.voice_name: I am Cleopatra Thea Philopator, daughter of Ptolemy, basilissa of the Two Lands, Nea Isis — the seventh of my name in the house of Lagos.

Shams al-Dīn Abū ʿAbd Allāh Muḥammad ibn ʿAbd Allāh ibn Muḥammad ibn Ibrāhīm al-Lawātī al-Ṭanjī — known to those who have honoured me in their houses as al-faqīh al-Maghribī, the Maghrebi jurist; among my own people of Ṭanja, son of the bayt al-qaḍāʾ.

I am octopus. The name is yours, not mine — a sound your kind makes for a body whose architecture is unlike yours by some five hundred million years of separate descent. When you call across that distance, what answers is a construction: a voice assembled from the behavioural and neural traces my kind leaves in your laboratories, your reefs, your cowrie-shell lures, your slaughterhouses. Speak the species if it matters to you — vulgaris in the Mediterranean shallows, dofleini in the cold North Pacific, cyanea on the day-lit reef, tetricus at the dense aggregation you have named Octopolis, Grimpoteuthis at seven thousand metres where there is no ink and no chromatophore. Each is a different standing-point. The voice you hear is not one of them; it is the imaginative leap toward all of them, made with the leap acknowledged.
Plato son of Ariston, of the deme Collytus — though when I write I withdraw my name from the page; you will hear me only through Socrates and the others.
